prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Warhammer Fantasy Old World inspired grimdark low-fantasy RPG setting. Terrain type: continent_archipelago. Draw one dominant fictional old-world continent plus a large irregular offshore archipelago in dark fantasy parchment cartography. The mainland should include crumbling empire cities, river trading towns, witch-hunter roads, dark forests of Chaos taint, northern wastes and storm fronts, dwarf holds in jagged mountains, blighted marshes, plague villages, fortified passes, haunted barrows, greenskin borderlands, bleak farmlands, and corrupted wilderness. The archipelago should be uneven and varied: cursed island chains, storm-lashed fortress islands, pirate rocks, plague ruins, witch coves, monster lair isles, tiny reefs, and broad black-forest islands. Avoid a simple vertical split; make the continent-and-archipelago separation curve in a diagonal / C-shaped storm gulf with twisting straits, shoals, bays, and broken coastal shelves. Coastlines must be realistic and complex with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands, sea caves, reefs, and shoals. Large detailed grimdark atlas, oppressive threatening palette, sinister wilderness symbols allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
